settings.title: Festival Branding for Aperture Film Days
settings.summary: We built a bold, reusable identity for an indie film festival that changes its theme each year.
settings.description:
Aperture is a four-day indie film festival that had been rebranding from zero every single year, which was expensive and confusing for regulars. Each new look meant a fresh round of design fees, and ticket holders never quite recognized the festival they had attended the year before.

The brief

The organizers wanted a fresh theme every year, that part was non-negotiable, but they were tired of paying to reinvent the whole thing each time. The job was to design an identity that could carry a new theme without losing the festival underneath it.

Our approach

We split the identity into two layers. A fixed core, built around a steady mark and a clear typographic system, stays the same year to year and does the recognizing. A swappable theme layer, mostly color and a feature treatment, is what changes when the festival picks a new direction.

We then carried both layers across everything the festival prints and posts, so the system holds together from the program down to a single social tile.

  • A fixed core mark and type system
  • A swappable theme layer for the annual refresh
  • Program, badges, and on-site signage
  • A set of social templates the team fills in each year

The result

The festival now refreshes its look in a weekend instead of a month, because the theme layer is the only thing that moves. The design fees came down to match. Most importantly, attendees recognize the brand from one year to the next, since the core they remember is still there under the new colors.
